key_points: Efficient transport networks reduce the cost and time of trade and improve economic productivity
Road transport complements railways by connecting remote regions to major transport networks
Ports act as gateways for international trade and connect India to global markets
Mass communication promotes national integration and spreads information quickly
Tourism has become an important economic activity generating employment and foreign exchange
examples: The Golden Quadrilateral highway project improves connectivity between major economic centres
Jawaharlal Nehru Port handles a large share of India's container trade
Inland waterways like the Ganga help transport heavy goods economically
Tourism in places like Goa and Rajasthan supports local industries
formulas: Balance of Trade = Total Exports − Total Imports
Unfavourable Balance of Trade occurs when Imports > Exports
exam_tips: Link transport development with economic growth in answers
Explain how communication systems help national integration
Use real examples like Golden Quadrilateral and major ports
Practice long-answer questions explaining the importance of different transport modes
